title: paraprasophyllum rogersii
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum rogersii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Ingestion of the plant's extracts can lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils or extracts can cause liver and kidney damage in rare cases. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can also lead to eye irritation and skin burns, especially if the plant's sap comes into contact with sensitive areas.
